description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a predominantly gray coloration and subtle variations in tone across its rough, uneven surface. The rock features a combination of angular and rounded edges, with one side appearing slightly flatter and broader, while the opposite side is more tapered and pointed. Fine mineral grains and subtle striations are visible, giving the rock a natural, weathered appearance. The surface texture is coarse and matte, with no visible polish or gloss, suggesting it has been exposed to the elements. There are no visible markings, carvings, or signs of artificial modification. Its size and form make it suitable as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A medium-sized, rough gray rock with angular edges and a coarse, matte surface, showing natural weathering and mineral striations.
description_view_0: The rock appears broad and squat, with a prominent flat face and visible mineral striations.
description_view_1: This side view reveals the rock's narrow, wedge-like profile and highlights its angular, pointed top.
description_view_2: From this angle, the rock looks more blocky and symmetrical, with a relatively flat surface facing forward.
description_view_3: The rock appears taller and more tapered, with a rough, uneven surface and less visible striation.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
